technical.focus: Implementation, code patterns, algorithm correctness, boundary conditions
key_findings: 3-tier system with hardcoded thresholds at 60%/90% — no hysteresis risk at boundary
4-level graduated eviction well-structured with early-exit after each level if budget satisfied
Circuit breaker with turn-based cooldown provides graceful degradation: ultra-compact → Pi default → lossy truncation
Snapshot-rollback via JSON deep clone isolates mutations from original message array
Content-aware token estimation (4 ratios) is more accurate than naive char/4
BUG: Level 4 add-back uses fixed initialTokens that never updates — budget can be exceeded
BUG: utils.ts is dead code — engine.ts has duplicated messageContent(), keyword patterns, error detection
BUG: No LRU cache despite CHANGELOG claims — estimateTokens is O(n) per call, called multiple times per compaction
BUG: Silent LLM failure catch — errors swallowed without logging
BUG: No quality scoring of generated summary
BUG: Dedup key only first 100 chars — false positives for messages with same prefix

architectural.focus: System design, scalability, module interactions, design patterns
key_findings: Clean inverted tree dependency graph: types ← (utils, engine) ← index — no circular dependencies
10 design patterns: Strategy (tiered), Circuit Breaker, Snapshot/Rollback, Graduated Eviction, Template Method, Factory, Observer, Ring Buffer, Dual-Gate Threshold, Adapter
engine.ts has ZERO Pi imports — pure compaction engine, independently testable and reusable
Type system well-designed (enums, interfaces) but UNDERUTILIZED — handlers use 'any' throughout despite typed interfaces existing
ENCAPSULATION BREACH: index.ts accesses engine['config'] via bracket notation for private fields
CODE DUPLICATION: messageContent(), keyword patterns, error detection duplicated between engine.ts and utils.ts
Module-level mutable state in index.ts (currentModel, compactionFailures, breakerTrippedAtTurn, currentTurn) — not encapsulated in a class
Pi API types defined but never used in implementation — documentation-only types
PiUI.notify level type mismatch: types say 'warn', implementation says 'warning'

domain_expert.focus: Compression domain best practices, context management standards, research alignment
key_findings: Hybrid sliding-window + summarization IS implemented and is the correct paradigm — aligns with LangChain ConversationSummaryBufferMemory
Token budget protection IS implemented (protectRecentByTokenBudget, 20K tokens) — matches RESEARCH.md recommendation
Tool output pruning saves 30-50% before LLM — aligns with research claim
CRITICAL: Importance scoring MISSING recency (0.30) and position (0.20) — the two HIGHEST weighted signals from RESEARCH.md
CRITICAL: Multi-pass summarization, quality scoring, LRU cache, entropy extraction — ALL ADVERTISED BUT NOT IMPLEMENTED
CRITICAL: compressMessage destroys code blocks (50-char stub) — directly contradicts code protection logic. Highest-impact domain anti-pattern for coding agent
BUG: Summary bloat — previous summary prepended verbatim, never compressed. Summaries grow across cycles, opposite of iterative-rewrite pattern
BUG: Budget enforcement in Level 4 eviction broken (initialTokens never updated)
BUG: shouldCompact uses hardcoded 0.6, not preemptiveWatermark (0.7) — 70% watermark is dead code in production
BUG: hardWatermark default 0.5 (code) vs 0.95 (README) — inverted, Gate 2 fires before Gate 1
NO semantic salience — regex-only despite RESEARCH.md flagging this as insufficient
NO hysteresis on tier boundary — MICRO/FULL thrash near 0.9
Extraction regex-anchored on explicit keywords (GOAL:, DECIDED:) — brittle to natural language
File path regex inconsistent: shouldProtectContent misses .py/.rs/.go that importance scorer recognizes

business_performance.focus: Token savings, performance optimization, user value, DX, Pi ecosystem
key_findings: Micro-compaction is genuinely zero-LLM and synchronous — claim verified
'Runs in microseconds' — UNVERIFIED (tests assert 10s/60s limits, not microseconds)
LRU cache claimed in CHANGELOG v0.6.0 but NOT in code — '3x faster' claim unsupported
hardWatermark: README says 0.95, code says 0.5 — INVERTED documentation error
shouldCompact() ignores thresholdTokens — user-configured thresholds stored but not consulted
preemptiveWatermark (0.7) is dead code in production — only used by shouldCompactDefaultThreshold() which is never called
Compression ratio baseline: 0.784 (21.6% savings) — far from claimed 70% in RESEARCH.md
Compact templates save 10-15% — unverified, no A/B test exists
337 tests with good coverage including edge cases and fuzz testing
Circuit breaker + lossy truncation provides genuine fault tolerance — tested
Model switching preserves userThresholdOverride — but it's not actually used by shouldCompact
Default heuristic mode has no API cost — favorable cost-benefit ratio
Cache-aware mode is sound but default-off — most users get no benefit
Lossy truncation tailKeep=10 is hardcoded and aggressive — not configurable

convergent_themes: All 4 perspectives confirm: core compaction pipeline (classify→protect→evict→summarize) is well-designed and aligns with mainstream context management
All 4 perspectives confirm: graduated eviction with early-exit is the strongest design quality
All 4 perspectives confirm: circuit breaker + snapshot-rollback provides genuine fault tolerance
All 4 perspectives confirm: documentation/implementation drift is severe — multiple advertised features do not exist in code
All 4 perspectives identify: Level 4 eviction budget enforcement bug (engine.ts:1050-1088)
All 4 perspectives identify: utils.ts is dead code from incomplete refactoring
All 4 perspectives identify: shouldCompact() uses hardcoded 0.6, ignoring configurable thresholds
conflicting_views: Technical agent: 'comprehensive boundary condition coverage' (positive) vs Domain expert: 'extraction brittle to natural language' (negative) — different scopes of boundary handling

unique_contributions.technical: Identified Level 4 add-back bug and silent LLM failure catch
unique_contributions.architectural: Identified 10 design patterns and encapsulation breach via bracket notation
unique_contributions.domain_expert: Identified code block destruction in compressMessage and summary bloat anti-pattern
unique_contributions.business: Identified LRU cache absence, hardWatermark inversion, and preemptiveWatermark dead code

technical_solutions.solution: Fix Level 4 budget enforcement — update running token count as candidates are added back
technical_solutions.problem: removeOldCompressibleMessages uses fixed initialTokens, never updates as candidates added
technical_solutions.rationale: Level 4 is the only eviction level meant to guarantee budget compliance
technical_solutions.alternatives: Could also add a post-pass token check that removes excess messages
technical_solutions.next_action: Implement running token counter in add-back loop

technical_solutions.solution: Fix shouldCompact to use preemptiveWatermark instead of hardcoded 0.6
technical_solutions.problem: shouldCompact uses hardcoded 0.6 multiplier, ignoring preemptiveWatermark config
technical_solutions.rationale: 70% preemptive watermark is advertised but dead code in production
technical_solutions.alternatives: Could also fix hardWatermark default from 0.5 to 0.95 to match README

technical_solutions.solution: Fix compressMessage to preserve code block structure instead of 50-char stub
technical_solutions.problem: compressMessage replaces entire code blocks with 50 chars, destroying protected content
technical_solutions.rationale: For a coding agent use case, code is the most valuable content to preserve
technical_solutions.alternatives: Keep first+last N lines, or use a hash-based reference
technical_solutions.next_action: Redesign code block compression to retain structural information

technical_solutions.solution: Implement summary merge/condense to prevent bloat across cycles
technical_solutions.problem: Previous summary prepended verbatim, re-extracted, causing monotonic growth
technical_solutions.rationale: RESEARCH.md:66-71 shows iterative rewrite, not stacking
technical_solutions.alternatives: Could cap summary size, or use LLM to merge old+new summaries
technical_solutions.next_action: Add merge step that condenses prior summary + new extraction

technical_solutions.solution: Complete utils.ts refactoring — make engine.ts import from utils.ts
technical_solutions.problem: Code duplication: messageContent(), keyword patterns, error detection exist in both files
technical_solutions.rationale: Divergent copies will drift, creating maintenance burden
technical_solutions.alternatives: Could also delete utils.ts if the refactoring is abandoned
technical_solutions.next_action: Replace engine.ts duplicates with imports from utils.ts
